recommendation_baseline: Maintain current settings and economizer operation while outdoor enthalpy/humidity remain favorable. Optionally increase OA fraction to further reduce compressor load if humidity and pressurization constraints allow; verify damper tracking and temperature sensor calibration during routine maintenance.
recommendation_improved: Maintain current operation; continue to monitor economizer control to maximize pre-cooling during mild conditions while observing humidity and minimum-OA requirements. Re-verify OA fraction and temperature readings during tune-ups given current values are self-consistent.

recommendation_baseline: Maintain current operation; no corrective action needed. If outdoor air humidity/enthalpy and indoor pressurization allow, consider increasing economizer OA fraction to further cut mechanical cooling (potential reduction on the order of ~18 kW_th if driven toward full OA under similar conditions). Continue trending SAT stability and fan kW/cfm to verify sustained performance.
recommendation_improved: Maintain current control settings and monitor the economizer to opportunistically increase OA when outdoor conditions permit while maintaining humidity/pressure targets. Continue maintaining filtration and fan setup to sustain ~0.33 W/cfm performance.

recommendation_baseline: Enable and test economizer cooling now: command OA damper to maximum and verify full stroke and feedback; sequence to use OA up to the SAT limit, then add mechanical. Review economizer enable/lockouts (enthalpy/humidity, low SAT/coil freeze, occupancy/schedule) and relax if overly conservative. Calibrate OAT/RAT/MAT and verify OA airflow measurement. Inspect damper/linkage/actuator and confirm adequate building relief to avoid over-pressurization at high OA. Trend SAT, MAT, OAT, OA fraction, and mechanical cooling for 1–2 days; target OA fraction well above 0.29 during cool outdoor periods while maintaining the 55°F SAT.
recommendation_improved: Review and tune economizer enable/high-limit settings and sequence so OA increases when OAT < RAT in cooling. Functionally test OA dampers (command to high OA and verify response) and, if capable, allow higher OA during similar conditions; trend OAT/MAT/SAT to confirm pre-cooling and reduced coil load.
